06 March 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Production Update - Correction
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following correction to the RNS of 6.3.26 regarding the production update on its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Whereas the stated oil production is correct with the C-64 and C-74 wells having produced at a March-to-date average of 195 BOPD, the C-77H well is producing on plateau at circa
50,000
scfd.

latest.title: Cambay PSC Production Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5656V
Synergia Energy Ltd
06 March 2026
RNS Announcement
06 March 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Production Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Production from the Cambay field saw a marked improvement from two legacy wells (C-64 and C-74) that were worked over in November 2025. Combined production from the C-64 and C-74 wells averaged 78 BOPD during February. Following recent increases in pump rates, the two wells have produced a March-to-date average of 195 BOPD. The C-77H gas well continues to produce on plateau at circa 500,000 scfd.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Cambay PSC Sale Agreement Update
latest.content:
Today 13:29
RNS Number : 2872S
Synergia Energy Ltd
09 February 2026
RNS Announcement
09 February 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Sale Agreement Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
On 1 December 2025 Synergia and Antelopus Selan Energy Limited ("
Selan
") signed a Sale and Purchase Agreement ("
SPA
") on terms previously announced, with a total consideration of up to US$14 million payable in two tranches. The SPA was conditional on Synergia shareholder approval and Selan establishing a bank guarantee for the deferred payment tranche. Synergia shareholder approval was gained at the 29 December 2025 Synergia AGM.
Selan has failed to provide the required bank guarantee for the deferred tranche citing refusal by Selan's major shareholder, Oak Tree Capital, to sanction completion of the SPA.
Under the Heads of Terms signed by both parties on 4 July 2025, Selan had exclusivity until 8 February 2026. Synergia remains willing to complete the SPA, however, since the conditions precedent for the SPA have not been satisfied by Selan prior to 8 February 2026, Synergia intends to examine its options. In the event that the SPA is not completed, Selan's non-refundable payment of US $0.5 million made under the Heads of Terms will not be returned.
Roland Wessel, Synergia's CEO stated:
"
After 9 months of protracted negotiations, Selan has not satisfied the conditions precedent within the exclusivity period to complete
the transaction. We believe in the inherent value of the Cambay PSC's 206 BCF of proven P50 Eocene gas reserves. The parties remain bound by their obligations under the previously executed Farm-In/Farm-Out Agreement including the agreed work program and in particular the drilling of three new wells in the Eocene."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel

latest.title: Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8566P
Synergia Energy Ltd
22 January 2026
RNS Announcement
22 January 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Cambay C78 Well Status:
Subsequent to the drilling and casing operations, the C-78 well was perforated over a 5 meter interval in the Oligocene OSII zone. The well showed gas during clean up and testing operations. The well is currently under observation to monitor pressure behaviour, which will help in subsequent technical studies and operational planning for future gas monetisation from C-78.
Workover Operations:
As a result of the workover operations, production has stabilised at an aggregate level of circa 40 bopd (gross) from the C-64 and C-74 wells.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Results of General Meeting
latest.content:
29 Dec 2025 11:22
RNS Number : 0003N
Synergia Energy Ltd
29 December 2025
For a printer friendly copy of this announcement, please click on the link below to open a PDF version.
http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0003N_1-2025-12-29.pdf
RNS Announcement
29 December 2025
AIM: SYN
Results of General Meeting
Synergia Energy Ltd (the
"
Company"
) is pleased to announce the voting results from the Company's General Meeting of Shareholders, held on Monday, 29 December 2025.
The Company advises that all motions put to the meeting were voted on by poll, with resolution one carried, resolution two was not passed. Details of the proxy voting and voting outcomes for all motions put to the meeting are set out in the attached report which can be accessed through the pdf link above.
Accordingly, and as previously advised, the Company will execute the Sale and Purchase Agreement and submit a Deed of Assignment to the Government of India to transfer the Company's 50% working interest in the Cambay PSC to Selan.
The Transaction remains subject to approval from the Government of India and subject to Selan establishing a bank guarantee for the deferred payment in a format suitable for the Company.
Following the Disposal,
The Company will re-focus its activities and will:
·
strive to progress the UK Medway Hub Camelot CCS project and install a new joint venture partner to replace Harbour Energy on the CS019 Camelot CCS licence;
·
seek to manage the return of capital to shareholders via a share buy-back scheme;
·
the India Project Office will manage the exit from the Cambay PSC and recovery of proceeds and withholding taxes;
·
progress the Cambay Basin CCS Scheme with regulatory authorities and the Government of India; and
·
seek to identify additional oil and gas development opportunities, primarily in India leveraging off the experience and expertise of the Company's management team.
In accordance with the result of the shareholder vote, the Company will no longer proceed with the planned delisting of the Company's shares on AIM at the present time and will focus on developing the Company's business and returning value to shareholders while remaining an AIM traded company.
Further announcements will be made as appropriate.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Ltd
Allison Pacinotti
Company Secretary

company_30s.what_they_do: SYN.L is an oil‑and‑gas exploration outfit that owns a handful of production‑concession agreements (PSCs) in the Cambay basin of India. Its core activity is drilling, testing and developing these wells to bring hydrocarbons to market.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The Cambay basin is one of the world’s fastest‑growing oil regions, and the company’s PSCs sit on a basin that’s recently seen a surge in oil prices and a renewed interest from foreign investors. If the wells start producing, the company could become a key supplier to the Indian domestic market and a source of steady cash flow for its shareholders.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the early development stage with no proven production yet. Cash burn is high as the company spends on drilling and regulatory approvals, and it has not yet generated significant revenue. It trades at a very small fraction of its book value, reflecting the uncertainty around a commercial turnaround.

rns_narrative: The company’s most recent filings show a focus on cash‑generating moves: a sale agreement for a Cambay PSC that will inject fresh capital, routine updates on AGM outcomes, and a series of governance documents that clarify ownership and advisory structures.

These events cluster into three themes: (1) Asset monetisation to shore up liquidity, (2) Strengthening corporate governance to regain investor confidence, and (3) Routine financial reporting that keeps the market informed of its fiscal health.

The sale of a PSC indicates the company is actively seeking ways to reduce debt and build a runway for drilling. The AGM updates suggest a push to align shareholders around a long‑term play, while the financial reports confirm that the company is still operating at a loss.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet under‑the‑surface: low chatter, a mix of cautious optimism about gas prices and worry over cash burn.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders highlight the recent spike in oil and gas prices, arguing that the company’s wells could become profitable if production starts, especially with the new PSC sale bringing in capital.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many observers point to the company’s heavy losses, lack of production, and the fact that the PSC sale only provides a temporary cash cushion. They fear the firm may still be unable to cover its operating costs and could face delisting if it fails to turn profitable.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the last month the chatter has moved from neutral to slightly bearish, driven by a series of losses and the slow pace of drilling progress. However, the recent PSC sale announcement has injected a glimmer of hope that could tilt sentiment back toward optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker are flat, indicating no current public interest and a high potential for surprise when a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: The formal announcement of the PSC sale, which should provide a clear cash injection and reduce debt levels.
A drilling milestone – such as a successful completion test or a first‑production report – that signals the wells are ready to deliver hydrocarbons.
A sustained rise in global oil prices above $80 a barrel, which would improve the economics of the Cambay basin and make production more attractive.
Any regulatory approval that clears the company to begin commercial production, which could trigger a buying frenzy.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 20‑30% volume spike above the 20‑day average, a breakout above the 3‑month low, or a headline confirming that the PSC sale has closed. A sudden increase in institutional ownership or a change in the company’s credit rating would also be a positive sign.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a post‑crash recovery. The APEX score suggests a tight price range, signalling that a breakout could happen soon if the right catalyst arrives. Until then, the market remains in a holding pattern, waiting for a clear sign that the company is moving out of capitulation.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has dropped well below its peak, giving a 70–95% crash that historically yields large rebounds. If the PSC sale is successful and production starts, the price could climb to roughly halfway back to its former level – a 150–200% upside from today’s floor. This aligns with the RED signal’s 162.8% average return and 56.9% win rate.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The firm still has no proven production and is operating at a loss. If the PSC sale fails to cover operating costs, the company could face a liquidity crunch, delisting, or even bankruptcy. In that scenario, the downside could be a complete wipe‑out of the equity value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: a 20% drop from the current floor. Potential gain: a 150% rise if the price recovers to halfway. The upside‑to‑downside ratio is about 7.5:1, a classic asymmetric trade that favours the contrarian.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 3‑month low and volume is at least 1.5 times the 20‑day average. Allocate about 5% of your portfolio to the position, set a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false breakout, and consider scaling in as the price consolidates.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash or a small position until you see a confirmed PSC sale closing, a first‑production report, or a significant oil price rally. Monitor the volume and price for a breakout above the 3‑month low – that’s your entry trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, is delisted, or the PSC sale falls through and the debt load remains unsustainable, exit immediately and avoid further losses.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash combined with a fresh PSC sale and rising oil prices creates a high‑reward, low‑downside opportunity for contrarians ready to act when the first signs of production appear.
